    All Classes and Interfaces

    Class
    Description
    BasePuzzle
    The BasePuzzle class is an abstract blueprint for all puzzle types in the game.
    CustomPuzzlePane
    The CustomPuzzlePane class provides a user interface for managing and creating custom puzzles.
    CustomPuzzlePane.ImageHintBox
    Represents a container for a selected puzzle image and its associated hint field.
    DigitalTimer
    The DigitalTimer class represents a visual countdown clock in the game UI.
    Easy
    The Easy class represents the basic difficulty level of a puzzle in the game.
    GameMode
    The GameMode enum defines the available difficulty levels and play styles within the application.
    GameModeSelector
    The GameModeSelector class is responsible for managing the difficulty state of the game.
    GamePane
    The GamePane class is responsible for displaying the puzzle images and their associated hints during a gameplay session.
    Goto
    The Goto class serves as the primary utility and navigation controller for the application.
    Goto.CustomPuzzleEntry
    A record of a custom puzzle entry including its unique ID, difficulty level, and puzzle instance.
    Hard
    The Hard class represents the most challenging difficulty level for a puzzle.
    hasHint
    The hasHint interface is designed for puzzle objects that provide progressive clues.
    hasLife
    The hasLife interface defines the core life-management capabilities for entities.
    LifePane
    LifePane is a UI component that displays the player's name and their remaining health points (HP).
    Main
    The Main class serves as the entry point for the JavaFX application.
    Medium
    The Medium class represents an intermediate difficulty level for a puzzle.
    ModeSelectionPane
    ModeSelectionPane is a UI component that provides the interface for players to select their desired game mode (EASY, MEDIUM, HARD, or BATTLE).
    Player
    The Player class represents a participant in the game.
    ResultPane
    ResultPane is a UI component that displays the game over screen or final results.
    RootPane
    The RootPane class serves as the primary container for the entire application's UI.
    TextPane
    The TextPane class provides a user interface component for players to input their answers.
    TitleScreenPane
    The TitleScreenPane class represents the initial landing page of the game.
